title: Methods and devices for network energy saving in a wireless communication system considering a local load
abstract: The present disclosure relates to methods and devices for saving energy on a radio network access side of a wireless communication system.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A method (40) for transmitting control messages in a wireless communication system, wherein the wireless communication system comprises a macro base station, BS, and a plurality of small BSs located in a coverage of the macro-BS, wherein the macro-BS (100) transmits, for the plurality of small BSs, control messages to user equipment, UEs, in respective coverages of the plurality of small BSs (110), wherein the method is implemented by a small BS among the plurality of small BSs and comprises: evaluating (S40) whether a local load of the small-BS, due to the UEs in its coverage, verifies a local overload criterion, responsive to the local overload criterion being verified: informing (S41 ) the macro- BS that the local overload criterion is verified, responsive to receiving, from the macro-BS, an instruction to start transmitting all of part of the control messages: transmitting (S43) all or part of the control messages, previously transmitted by the macro-BS, to UEs in the coverage of the small BS. 2. The method (40) according to claim 1 , comprising, after the small BS starts transmitting all or part of the control messages to UEs in its coverage: - evaluating (S44) whether the local load of the small-BS, due to the UEs in its coverage, verifies a local normal load criterion, - responsive to the local normal load criterion being verified: informing (S45) the macro-BS that the local normal load criterion is verified, - responsive to receiving, from the macro-BS, an instruction to stop transmitting all or part of the control messages: interrupting (S47) the transmission of all or part of the control messages to UEs in the coverage of the small BS. 3. The method (40) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the local overload criterion is verified if the number of UEs in the coverage of the small BS is greater than a first threshold. 4. The method (40) according to claim 2, wherein the local overload criterion is verified if the number of UEs in the coverage of the small BS becomes greater than a first threshold and the local normal load criterion is verified if the number of UEs in the coverage of the small BS becomes lower than a second threshold, wherein the second threshold is lower than or equal to the first threshold. 5. The method (40) according to claim 3 or 4, wherein the first threshold is configured by the macro-BS and/or the second threshold is configured by the macro-BS. 6. A method (50) for transmitting control messages in a wireless communication system, wherein the wireless communication system comprises a macro base station, BS, Internal 

and a plurality of small BSs located in a coverage of the macro-BS, wherein the macro-BS (100) transmits, for the plurality of small BSs, control messages to user equipment, UEs, in respective coverages of the plurality of small BSs (110), wherein the method is implemented by the macro-BS and comprises: - receiving (S50), from a small BS, an indication that a local load of the small BS, due to the UEs in the coverage of said small BS, verifies a local overload criterion, - evaluating (S52) whether a global load of the macro-BS, due to the UEs and/or to the plurality of small BSs in the coverage of the macro-BS, verifies a global overload criterion, responsive to the global overload criterion being verified: instructing (S51) the small BS from which an indication has been received to start transmitting all or part of the control messages, previously transmitted by the macro-BS, to UEs in its coverage. 7. The method (50) according to claim 6, comprising: receiving (S53), from the small BS which has started to transmit all or part of the control messages to UEs in its coverage, an indication that the local load of the small BS verifies a local normal load criterion, - instructing (S54) said small BS to stop transmitting all or part of the control messages to UEs in its coverage. 8. The method (50) according to any one of claims 6 to 7, wherein the global overload criterion is verified for the macro-BS if the number of small BSs in its coverage is greater than a first threshold and/or if the number of UEs in its coverage is greater than a second threshold and/or if the number of UEs in the coverages of the plurality of small BSs is greater than a third threshold. 9. The method (50) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the control messages transmitted by the macro-BS, for the plurality of small BSs, include system information block, SIB, messages, synchronization signal/physical broadcast channel block, SSB, messages and paging messages, and transmitting all or part of the control messages by a small BS corresponds to transmitting at least one among SIB messages, SSB messages and paging messages. 10. A computer program product comprising instructions which, when executed by at least one processor, configure said at least one processor to carry out a method (40) according to any one of claims 1 to 5 and/or a method (50) according to any one of claims 6 to 9. 11. A computer-readable storage medium comprising instructions which, when executed by at least one processor, configure said at least one processor to carry out a Internal 

method (40) according to any one of claims 1 to 5 and/or a method (50) according to any one of claims 6 to 9. 12. A base station, BS, (120) comprising at least one memory (122) and at least one processor (121) configured to carry out a method (40) according to any one of claims 1 to 5 and/or a method (50) according to any one of claims 6 to 9. 13. Wireless communication system comprising a macro base station, BS, and a plurality of small BSs located in a coverage of the macro-BS, wherein the macro-BS (100) transmits, for the plurality of small BSs, control messages to user equipment, UEs, in respective coverages of the plurality of small BSs (110), wherein: - the macro-BS comprises at least one memory and at least one processor configured to carry out a method (50) according to any one of claims 6 to 9, each small BS comprises at least one memory and at least one processor configured to carry out a method (40) according to any one of claims 1 to 5.
